steps.title: Identify the causal relationship
steps.content: The passage establishes that Castle Geyser erupts at regular intervals (10–12 hours apart), then claims that predicting its eruptions is feasible. The transition must signal that the second idea results from the first — regularity enables predictability. This is a causal or consequential link.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: Consequently expresses the causal relationship — it ties predictability directly to the established regularity of eruptions. Conversely, Nevertheless, and Additionally introduce contrast, concession, or mere addition respectively, none of which fit the causal logic. This matches choice D — D is correct.
